title: He Ran 50K Then Raced 106 Miles at Mid South
content:
Reid Burrows took on one of the most demanding challenges in gravel racing: a 50K run followed by a 106-mile race at Mid South, one of the most iconic events in the discipline. This is the Pro Double, an effort that pushes the limits of endurance, equipment, decision-making, and the constant self-talk that comes with it.

This is the first film in “Within the Margin of Error,” a new project from Silca exploring the people and moments that shape cycling.

After years chasing performance, from triathlon to ultra running and success at the Leadville Trail 100, Reid is redefining what success looks like. This season is not just about podiums. It is about curiosity, community, and building something on his own terms.

The post He Ran 50K Then Raced 106 Miles at Mid South appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
